title: Claude 2026 Prompting and Claude Code Practice · Eval Recall
summary: Apply current Anthropic guidance to tool use, examples, context, and long-running coding. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a Claude-specific prompt and eval suite after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use clear direct instructions, motivation, and relevant context for current Claude models.
Use diverse examples and structural tags when they improve task boundary recognition.
Steer tool selection with descriptions and instructions, using enforced tool choice only when required.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt claude practice. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Claude Code Context trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for provide claude code repository guidance, verification commands, permissions, and durable project context.

content.text: For Claude Code Context, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Claude Code Context precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt claude practice. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Claude Code needs only a feature request to modify any repository safely.

stimulus.title: Claude Code Context case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt claude practice. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Claude Code needs only a feature request to modify any repository safely.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Provide Claude Code repository guidance, verification commands, permissions, and durable project context. Artifact under review: a Claude-specific prompt and eval suite.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Claude Code Context, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Claude Code Context applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Provide Claude Code repository guidance, verification commands, permissions, and durable project context.
